subject: [SO] Bump container versions to 1.7.10
commit_message:
[SO] Bump container versions to 1.7.10

SO 1.7.10 containers are release containers

so-bpmn-infra: 1.7.10
so-catalog-db-adapter: 1.7.10
so-monitoring: 1.7.10
so/nssmf-adapter: 1.7.10
so/openstack-adapter: 1.7.10
so/request-db-adapter: 1.7.10
so/sdc-controller: 1.7.10
so/sdnc-adapter: 1.7.10
so/vnfm-adapter:1.7.10
so/api-handler-infra:1.7.10

Issue-ID: SO-3346
Change-Id: I450734f8b4704c767c330deff8a830ae84ba3d2c
Signed-off-by: Kalkere Ramesh, Sharan <sk720x@att.com>

Still CNF orchestration looks like a real issue, and not an environment problem...
@Andreas?

Patch Set 2:

yep
Thierry detected a nice SO java exception here: https://logs.onap.org/onap-integration/gating//114892-2/infrastructure-healthcheck/k8s-teardown/kubernetes-status/pod-onap-so-bpmn-infra-54f6db44f8-jfggh-so-bpmn-infra-logs.html
getting a confirmation woule be helpful to see if it is reproducible and introduced by 1.7.10 (never got it with 1.7.9)

regarding the redeployment, do not use the "magic word" too often
you can see now 2 queues are indicated in the gerrit logs
the first  (1 then 3) corresponds to the experimental gating (helm3 / storage classes / )
the second (16 then 15) 
depending on the number of available gating labs (from 0 to 6 - currently 2) it may take some time
assuming we have currently 2 available labs => ~ gating in round(15/2) * 2H = 14h
no need to ask for a new deployment, no resources are available, request will just be put in queue
